MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues/ Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Apr-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Malcolm Leader-Picone, of Bartlett, Leader-Picone & Young, LLP, Tel. 510.444.2404, for plaintiffs;
Jennifer L. Stark and Gay Grunfeld, of Rosen Bien Galvan & Grunfeld LLP, Tel. 415.433.6830, for defendants;
No other appearances.

RULING: Judge Kahn disclosed that he knows Cathy Rosen, wife of first named counsel for defendant, who passed away. This will not affect the ability of the Court to hear this matter. The matter was argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI AND AGESONG, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES is granted in part and denied in part. The first two causes of action for breach of contract and fraud in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") set forth two distinct wrongs under each cause of action - 1) the cancellation of Synergy LLC and 2) the refusal to perform under the Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") and Spirit Letter. Pursuant to Lilienthal & Fowler v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848, 1854-55, the Court will treat each distinct wrong as a separate cause of action for purposes of this motion. As to the causes of action for breach of contract and fraud as they pertain to the cancellation of Synergy LLC, summary adjudication is granted. Res judicata bars these claims, as they were explicitly addressed and adjudicated by the Final Award. (Declaration of Jennifer Stark, Ex. K, Final Award, p. 9.) As to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, summary adjudication is granted because res judicata bars the entire cause of action. (Id.) Summary adjudication is granted as to the fourth cause of action for tortious interference with contractual relationship. The undisputed evidence establishes that Plaintiffs are not holders of the bonds, are not express beneficiaries, and are not individual signatories to the agreements governing the bonds. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 81-110.) Accordingly, Plaintiffs do not have standing to maintain this cause of action in their individual capacities. As to the cause of action for fraud as it pertains to the refusal to perform under the MOU and Spirit Letter, summary adjudication is denied. Defendant did not meet his initial burden. As to the statute of limitations argument, Defendant does not submit any evidence about when the Plaintiffs discovered the fraud or any evidence about when Defendant's obligations under the MOU and Spirit Letter were triggered. (See UMF 69-80.) As to the argument that the fraud claim is meritless, Defendant does not present any evidence that Plaintiff cannot establish this cause of action. (See UMF 69-80.) A defendant moving for summary judgment on the grounds that an element of a cause of action cannot be established must "present evidence, and not simply point out, that the plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-55.) Because Defendant did not meet his initial burden as to the fraud cause of action, summary adjudication is denied as to the fifth cause of action for Violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 and the claim for punitive damages. Prevailing party shall submit form of order that complies with CCP 437C(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.
